Wikipedia Wittman Regional Airport
Wikipedia Wittman Regional Airport; Skyway Airlines served OSH

detail: EAS subsidy terminated March 1, 2003 under DOT Order 2003-2-20 because required subsidy exceeded $200/passenger, the statutory cap for communities within 210 miles of a medium hub (Milwaukee).
source: Wikipedia Wittman Regional Airport; DOT Order 2003-2-20

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Veterans Airport of Southern Illinois; Ozark DC-9 flights ended early 1982
Wikipedia Veterans Airport of Southern Illinois; Great Lakes Airlines served MWA until ~2019 before Contour took over EAS contract

detail: Ozark DC-3s began service 1955; DC-9 flights ended early 1982; EAS subsidized service continued through Great Lakes Airlines then Contour Aviation to ORD; annual EAS subsidy is $2,562,819 ($141/passenger).
source: Wikipedia Veterans Airport of Southern Illinois; veteransairport.com/history
